REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS DEBORAH NGUYEN, CHRISTIAN NGUYEN, AND VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FALSE DISCLOSURE STATEMENT, FRAUD, DECEIT AND NEGLIGENCE AGAINST THE MOVING DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT THESE DEFENDANTS WERE AWARE THAT THE EXCLUSIVE USE AGREEMENT HAS BEEN REVOKED PRIOR TO THE EXECUTION OF CONTRACT (COMPLAINT, PARAGRAPH 29). NEVERTHELESS MOVING DEFENDANTS MADE AFFIRMATIVE REPRESENTATIONS REGARDING THE EXCLUSIVE USE OF THE GARAGE AND CONCEALED THE TRUE FACTS REGARDING REVOCATION OF THE AGREEMENT. ADDITIONALLY, WHILE DEFENDANTS ARGUE THAT THEY WERE UNDER NO OBLIGATION TO PROVIDE INFORMATION BEYOND VISUAL INSPECTION, DEFENDANTS TOOK IT UPON THEMSELVES TO MAKE THE DISCLOSURES REGARDING THE GARAGE AND INCORPORATED BY REFERENCE SELLER'S REPRESENTATIONS. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS.) JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Motion To Strike Portions Of The Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
** Defendants' Motion to Strike and Demurrer to Complaint are heard concurrently.**

Appearances:
Robert N. Weaver, esq. (415-398-9800) for Plaintiff/opposing party Eric Linker.
Mark Wilson, esq. (415-400-0829) of Manning & Kass for Defendants/Moving party DEBORAH NGUYEN, CHRISTIAN NGUYEN, and VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.

Plaintiff submitted to tentative ruling. Defendant ARGUED re: seller's representation, garage is described as share space in CC&R, Nguyens are not principles/officers/directors of Vanguard, and Vanguard should not be held responsible. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs DEBORAH NGUYEN, CHRISTIAN NGUYEN, and VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'s Motion To Strike Portions Of The Complaint is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to the attorney's fees and prejudgment interest requests. Non-opposition filed. Motion is denied as to the request for punitive damages. Fraud is sufficiently alleged. Order is signed. (See Scanned Order for Details.) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
** Defendants' Motion to Strike and Demurrer to Complaint are heard concurrently.**

Appearances:
Robert N. Weaver, esq. (415-398-9800) for Plaintiff/opposing party Eric Linker.
Mark Wilson, esq. (415-400-0829) of Manning & Kass for Defendants/Moving party DEBORAH NGUYEN, CHRISTIAN NGUYEN, and VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.

Plaintiff submitted to tentative ruling. Defendant ARGUED re: seller's representation, garage is described as share space in CC&R, Nguyens are not principles/officers/directors of Vanguard, and Vanguard should not be held responsible. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs DEBORAH NGUYEN, CHRISTIAN NGUYEN, and VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Complaint sufficiently alleges causes of action for false disclosure statement, fraud, deceit and negligence against the moving defendants. Plaintiff alleges that these defendants were aware that the exclusive use agreement has been revoked prior to the execution of contract (Complaint, paragraph 29). Nevertheless moving defendants made affirmative representations regarding the exclusive use of the garage and concealed the true facts regarding revocation of the agreement. Additionally, while defendants argue that they were under no obligation to provide information beyond visual inspection, Defendants took it upon themselves to make the disclosures regarding the garage and incorporated by reference seller's representations. Order is signed. (See Scanned Order for Details.) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
